title: Device, kit and method for dental extraction in herbivorous quadrupeds
abstract: The present application relates to a dental extraction device for extracting teeth from herbivorous quadrupeds. The application further relates to a kit comprising the dental extraction device in combination with a drill bit. The application additionally relates to a method for extracting teeth from herbivorous quadrupeds.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A device for use in dental extraction comprising: (a) an elongated bearing plate comprising opposing first and second faces separated by the height of the bearing plate and opposing first and second ends separated by the length of the bearing plate, said bearing plate having formed therein a centrally disposed aperture in communication with the first and second faces; and (b) spaced apart first and second support legs extending from the second face of the bearing plate with the first support leg positioned proximal to the first end of the bearing plate and the second support leg positioned proximal to the second end of the bearing plate, each support leg terminating in a base, and each base having an angle between about 0 and about 30 degrees relative to an axis parallel to the first face of the bearing plate and oriented laterally across the width of the bearing plate; wherein the aperture is for allowing a threaded fastener to extend through the height of the bearing plate. 2. The device of claim 1 , wherein the aperture comprises a threaded fastener receiving channel extending laterally from an edge of the bearing plate. 3. The device of claim 1 , wherein the first and second support legs extend laterally across at least a substantial portion of the width of the bearing plate. 4. The device of claim 1 , wherein the angle of the base of each of the first and second support legs is between about 5 and about 20 degrees. 5. The device of claim 2, wherein the slope of the base of each of the first and second support legs is between about 7 and about 1 1 degrees. 6. The device of claim 1 , wherein at least a portion of at least one of the first and second support legs is releasably joined to the device. 7. The device of claim 1 , wherein at least one of the first and second support legs is height adjustable. 8. The device of claim 1 , wherein the base of at least one of the first and second support legs comprises a resilient material. 9. A kit comprising a device of claim 1 and a drill bit. 10. The kit of claim 9, wherein the drill bit comprises a connector portion that is compatible with a dental power float. 11. The kit of claim 10, wherein the shank of the drill bit comprises wrench rests to allow the bit to be turned by a wrench. 12. The kit of claim 10, further comprising one or more items selected from a screw, a nut, a tap, a burr, and a wrench. 13. A kit comprising a device of claim 2 and a drill bit, the drill bit comprising a shank terminating in a connector portion that is compatible with a dental power float. 14. A kit comprising a device of claim 5 and a drill bit, the drill bit comprising a shank terminating in a connector portion that is compatible with a dental power float. 15. A method for dental extraction comprising: (a) inserting a threaded fastener into a tooth of a herbivorous quadruped; (b) positioning an elongated bearing plate, said bearing plate having formed therein a centrally disposed aperture extending through the height of the bearing plate for receiving the threaded fastener, around the threaded fastener such that the bearing plate is supported by surfaces rostral and caudal to the tooth in an elevated position relative to the surface of the tooth; and (c) driving a nut axially along the threaded fastener towards and in abutment with a first face of the bearing plate, the first face of the bearing plate being the face of the bearing plate facing away from the tooth, thereby forcing translocation of the threaded fastener through the bearing plate, wherein translocation of the threaded fastener through the bearing plate pulls the tooth towards a second face of the bearing plate, the second face of the 

 bearing plate being the face of the bearing plate facing towards the tooth, thereby loosening the tooth from the periodontal ligament. 16. The method of claim 14 further comprising a step of drilling the tooth with a drill bit driven by a dental float prior to inserting the threaded fastener into the tooth. 17. The method of claim 14, wherein the bearing plate is comprised within a device of claim 1. 18. The method of claim 14, wherein the bearing plate is comprised within a device of claim 5. 19. The method of claim 14, wherein the herbivorous quadruped is an equid. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the equid is a horse.
